other_observers: None; I was the original finder of this bird, to my knowledge no one else saw it.
specific_location: On the SE side of Jumbo Reservoir. It was dam along the SE shore, just beyond the junction with CR 1.
description: A very brown bird. Brown back, brown belly, most of the head is brown (with some white on the head), white and brown chest, white and brown undertail coverts, dark brown to black primaries. Dark eye. Bicolored bill, base of the bill is pink and the outer half is black. The bill is also long and thin. Light pink legs. Very small bird, smaller than all the other birds that it was by. When I first saw it while I was driving by I could see all the brown and new it could be nothing else but a Mew Gull.Behaviors: Sitting on the ice with all the other gulls. Occasionally being flushed (by raptors and cars driving by), but then landing back on the ice.Call: Didn't knowingly hear itPlumage: 1st year
